issues_found: Source is extremely stale — published May 2016, nearly 10 years before TODAY (2026-04-12)
Specific inflation data from 2015 is historical context only and has no current macro catalyst relevance
quality_tier marked as 'low' is overly harsh — source is high-trust domain (Cleveland Fed) and content is accurate
human_review_reason: The record is factually accurate and well-sourced, but staleness creates a borderline decision. Under Core Rules, sources older than 90 days 'may still be accepted if it has clear archival value.' This speech has legitimate archival value as historical Fed thinking on inflation dynamics, the SIGMA model methodology reference, and context on the 2015-2016 below-target inflation period. The classification as 'speech_neutral' is correct. Human review needed to weigh archival value against current utility.

corrected_fields.why_it_matters: This speech provides historical context on how the Fed conceptualized its 2% symmetric inflation target and the challenges of achieving it during the prolonged post-Great Recession undershoot. The SIGMA model estimates on dollar passthrough remain relevant methodological reference points for understanding historical Fed research, though the specific 2015-2016 inflation data is historical context only. Appropriate as a historical research archive item documenting Fed thinking circa 2016.
